name: VgaRunsIndexPage

states.name: Page Structure & Layout
states.description: Top-level layout: a sticky header with back-to-/vga link, heading, and total badge; a scrollable main area with a filters card followed by the runs table card. The viewport height is clamped to `calc(100vh-44px)` so the inner main section scrolls independently of the app shell.

states.name: Filters Card
states.description: The filters card exposes four filters: status (Select), target_process (free-text Input with exact-match semantics), since (date Input), until (date Input), plus a Reset button. Changing any filter resets page to 1 and triggers a fresh /api/vga/runs query.

states.name: Runs Table
states.description: When runs exist, a 6-column table renders the current page of results: Run id (truncated to first 8 chars, linked to /vga/runs/[runId]), State machine name, Target process, Status badge, Started-at (locale datetime), Duration. Rows are ordered by started_at DESC.
states.precondition: At least one run row is rendered (data.runs.length > 0)

states.name: Run Status Badges
states.description: Each row's Status cell renders a Badge whose variant encodes status. The statusTone helper maps: succeeded|success→secondary, failed→destructive, drifted→outline, running→default, any other→outline. The label shown capitalizes the first letter except for unknown statuses which pass through raw.
states.precondition: At least one run with status in {succeeded, success} is visible on the current page

states.name: Loading / Empty / Error Lifecycle
states.description: The runs card renders one of four mutually exclusive sub-states based on the React Query status: isLoading → spinner, error → red error line, empty → muted 'No runs match these filters.', populated → table + pagination.
states.precondition: The page has just mounted and no data has arrived yet

states.name: Pagination Controls
states.description: When the table is populated, a footer row shows the current window and Prev/Next controls. The controls are disabled at the ends of the range. PAGE_SIZE is 50.
states.precondition: At least one run row is rendered (populated state)

states.name: URL ↔ Filter State Sync
states.description: The four filters plus the page index are mirrored into the URL query string. A shared link or bookmark must reproduce the exact filtered view. Sync is bidirectional via a single `lastSyncedRef` to prevent the state→URL effect from stomping params the user navigated in with.
states.precondition: The page is loaded with no query string

states.name: GET /api/vga/runs contract
states.description: The page is backed by the Next.js route handler at /api/vga/runs which executes a single SELECT over runner.vga_runs LEFT JOIN runner.vga_state_machines. The contract is observable from the page: the table's column values come directly from the row shape, totals come from COUNT(*) OVER(), filter params shape the WHERE clause.
states.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

states.name: Visual Design & Typography
states.description: Typography and color follow the theme tokens (bg-background, border-border, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground). The page header uses the same layout pattern as /vga to keep the two pages visually coherent.
states.precondition: At least one run row is rendered

states.name: Accessibility
states.description: Semantic HTML (h1, table with th scope, labeled form controls), aria-labels on icon-only buttons, keyboard-focus affordances.
states.precondition: At least one run row is rendered (populated state)

description: Paginated index of VGA (Visual GUI Automation) runs. Shows one row per row of runner.vga_runs joined to runner.vga_state_machines on state_machine_id. Columns: truncated run id linking to /vga/runs/[runId], state machine name, target process, status badge, started-at timestamp, duration. Supports filtering by status, target_process (exact match), and started_at date range (since/until). Filter state is mirrored into the URL query string so a reload or shared link restores the view. Pagination: 50 rows/page; total row count is computed server-side via COUNT(*) OVER() in the same query as the page slice. This page is the user's window into the Python VGA worker's execution history — the canonical place to see which runs succeeded, failed, drifted, or are still running for a given app.
